original_prompt:
by "end to end" i meant: follow a project through *completely* from brainstorm --> final paper. if the paper fails review and gets kicked back to an earliar stage, you still need to continue to shepard it through to verify that the pipeline and existing agents correctly address issues, revise, and so on.

so this task is not complete yet.

regarding code not running, it is worth verifying several things:
 - the "brainstorm" and "flesh out" agents should take into consideration that this work is not just about "anything" nor can "any" plan whatsoever be executed. it needs to fit within the scope of what can reasonably be expected to be accomplished using this pipeline. so extremely long run-times that can't be broken down into smaller tasks, or jobs that require powerful computing clusters beyond what's available in standard github actions, or data collection (beyond downloading existing datasets), are all out of scope.
 - we *do* need sufficient tool support to be able to execute code. otherwise we aren't going to be able to generate figures or do *any* sort of first-order research. without code execution all of the reserach products become simply literature reviews + speculation, rather than hard scientific projects that can truly advance knowledge.
